Although many times we think of gifts as tangible items like birthday presents and other objects, intangible things are frequently more important and effective. And unlike tangible gifts, they're the ultimate renewable resource. Take a moment now to think of a few intangible yet effective gifts you have given proactively to others in your past. Here's a list of some intangible gifts you can consider as a guide to use with people with whom you'd like to initiate or enhance a relationship. A key insight is that if you are seeking more information or positivity or trust inside a relationship, you can get it by giving it first. By the rule of reciprocity, it's likely to flow back to you. Okay, we've thrown a lot of information at you. So take a moment now and reflect upon how you can make sure that all your intangible or tangible gifts are really seen as significant, tailored, and surprising to the person you want to build a relationship with. Now take a moment to think about a person with whom you want to nurture a relationship. What intangible gift can you give this week to that person who would sincerely appreciate it and consider it a pleasant surprise? Write that down in your personal action plan.
